items.title: Missing royalties labelled 'petty cash' for Gina Rinehart after court ruling

children.content: Gina Rinehart and her company, Hancock Prospecting, are the "clear winners" of what prominent Perth industry commentator Tim Treadgold has declared the "big daddy" of legal battles.

articles.title: Here's what you need to know about the Gina Rinehart court ruling

children.content: The blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

articles.title: Split decision as Gina Rinehart, Wright family both claim legal wins

children.content: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

image.altText: A woman in a black jacket and pink shirt steps on stage

gallery.altText: A woman sits next to a man at a conference.

A woman in a hardhat speaks from a podium.

items.title: Man, 53, charged over seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants

image.altText: Photos of a hydronic cannabis operation supplied by police.

children.content: A 53-year-old Kalgoorlie man is released on bail after the seizure of more than 350 cannabis plants from a property WA Police allege had been converted for the purpose of growing hydroponic cannabis.

items.title: Containers for Change set to include wine bottles, but industry unhappy

children.content: A newly formed West Australian wine group warns that bottles of wine produced in the state could increase by $1.25 if the expansion of the Containers for Change scheme goes ahead.

thumbnail.altText: Red wine bottles on a shelf.
A dark-haired woman in a blazer holds a bottle of wine while standing in a vineyard.

items.title: Racist comments on WA Police social media posts remain online for days

children.content: Racist comments are being published on WA Police social media posts with little moderation, but the force says it is not prepared to switch off comments.

image.altText: A photo over someone's shoulder of them holding a phone with the WA Police Force facebook page on it.

items.title: Kerr scores landmark goal as Matildas win FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

image.altText: Female football players on the podium, captain in centre lifting trophy

items.title: Food minister warns of challenging times for WA consumers at checkout

children.content: WA Agriculture and Food Minister Jackie Jarvis says natural disasters as well as fuel and fertiliser availability and cost increases are contributing to real concerns around food supply in WA.

gallery.altText: Jackie wears a green top and brown jacket as she stands in an outdoor setting
A woman shopping for fresh produce in a supermarket.

thumbnail.altText: Jackie wears a green top and brown jacket as she stands in an outdoor setting
A woman shopping for fresh produce in a supermarket.

items.title: Unemployment rate remains at 4.3pc in 'calm before the storm'

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

items.title: Is LIV Golf dead? Not yet, but the vultures are circling

children.content: Rumours of LIV Golf's immediate demise look wide of the mark, but the smoke is only growing thicker as denials from the league's CEO help paint a bleak picture.

image.altText: Cameron Smith at a LIV Golf press conference

items.title: 'Into the hands of Trump': Dan Duggan's bid to avoid extradition fails

children.content: Former United States Marine Corps pilot Dan Duggan’s Federal Court bid to avoid extradition from Australia is dismissed.

image.altText: Former US military pilot Daniel Edmund Duggan seen as a pilot in Tasmania

items.title: Unemployment remains at 4.3pc, ASX closes lower — as it happened

children.content: The ASX closes lower after the release of ABS labour force data showing the unemployment remains at 4.3 per cent in seasonally adjusted terms. Viva Energy is in a trading halt after a fire at its Geelong oil refinery.

items.title: Why Australia turned to tiny nation to secure critical fertiliser supplies

children.content: The Middle East conflict has disrupted the flow of Australia's imports of the fertiliser urea. Facing the potential threat of reduced crop yields, Anthony Albanese looked to Australia's neighbours.

gallery.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

thumbnail.altText: Anthony Albanese in a grey and green jacket and white hard hat, smiling while holding up two jars of blue and brown urea.

items.title: AFL 'firestorm' taking heavy toll on players, Saints coach says

children.content: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon says the damage from a "firestorm" around the AFL Tribunal is weighing heavily as his club signals it may appeal against Lance Collard's suspension.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL coach stands on the ground looking downfield ahead of a match.

items.title: Quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and ER?

children.content: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor ER?

image.altText: Composite image of Noah Wyle as a young and older doctor

items.title: Rebel Wilson's The Deb is a delight despite the ongoing legal drama

children.content: Plus a powerful look Palestinian life through multiple generations and a buzzy Japanese video game adaptation makes it to Australian shores.

image.altText: Three women stand in front of a stage with blue and red velvet curtains

items.title: Creators of fuel dashboard quoted by News Corp have links to Liberals

children.content: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

image.altText: A graph showing a plane, a fuel pump and a plane with a countdown in red.

items.title: Amid chronic nursing shortages in country WA, Hamish is showing what it's like

children.content: Content creator Hamish Briggs is sharing his journey working as a nurse in Western Australia's far north, as the healthcare sector grapples with workforce shortages in regional areas.

items.title: Dash cam video of near-misses on WA highways prompts road safety call

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

items.title: Proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyer scheme slammed

children.content: Multicultural groups are concerned Angus Taylor's proposal to prevent permanent residents from accessing the Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme could deter skilled migrants from coming to Australia.

image.altText: Mr taylor, in suit and tie, speaks to the media in a passage at Parliament House.

items.title: Perth man on trial for alleged rape, assault of several women

children.content: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

image.altText: A medium shot with a sign reading: District Court of Western Australia.

items.title: Perth GP suspended for helping patient use $18,500 in super on cosmetic lipo

children.content: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

image.altText: A doctor and nurse in an operating room in a hospital.

items.title: WA children to get needle-free flu vaccine after worst season on record

children.content: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

gallery.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine

thumbnail.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine
